title: oenothera tubifera subsp. macrocarpa
intro: Oenothera tubifera subsp. macrocarpa, also known as the evening primrose or evening star, is a plant species native to the prairies and open woods of North America. It is a perennial herb with showy yellow flowers, which bloom in the evening and wilt with the morning sun. The roots, leaves, and flowers of the plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. Oenothera tubifera subsp. macrocarpa can be prepared as a tincture, salve, or tea to utilize its medicinal properties and provide health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing.
